core_behavior.note: Model B specifies a rigid 4-step linear flow with express checkout options and detailed UX rules; Model A lists general capabilities; Model C describes a high-level seamless flow — all three independently assumed different technical designs for an undefined UX requirement. Neither 'smooth' nor 'conversion-optimized' is given a technical definition by any model.

inputs.note: Model B specifies granular field-level validation rules, formats, and system inputs (fraud score, tax service); Model A and Model C list high-level input categories without field-level constraints.

outputs.note: Model C includes inventory update and transaction data sent to payment gateway as outputs; Model B specifies a 60-second email SLA and order record to OMS; Model A includes error messages as outputs — each model independently introduced different output artifacts.

assumptions.note: Model B has an empty assumptions array while Models A and C populate it with checkout flow structure, payment gateway, and device assumptions.

failure_modes.note: Model B has an empty failure_modes array while Models A and C specify payment errors, invalid input, network errors, and inventory failures.

edge_cases.note: Model B has an empty edge_cases array while Models A and C specify cases such as empty cart, abandoned checkout, expired payment methods, and connectivity issues.

security_constraints.note: Model B specifies only PAN and CVV storage prohibitions; Model A adds PCI compliance, access controls, and dependency patching; Model C adds encryption at rest and authentication for saved payment methods.

performance_constraints.note: Model A specifies 2-3 second response time, 100 concurrent sessions, and 99.9% uptime; Model B specifies only a 60-second email delivery SLA; Model C specifies 3-second page load and scalability for peak loads.

summary: ASM (Assumption Divergence) was the most frequently assigned category, appearing in 4 of 8 fields, reflecting that the vague requirement caused models to independently introduce incompatible design choices across core behavior, inputs, outputs, security, and performance.
